title: Precondition (f) defense-surface declared — Honesty triad extension ω-cycle
origin_context: Prior ω-cycle (2026-04-26_honesty_triad_refresh) found 3-of-4 repos have some defense doc but each in a different shape (tool/state/doc). Ω-extension proposed adding optional precondition (f) 'defense surface declared' to normalize the signal.

raw_admissibility.raw_73: cross-repo audit precondition — non-trivial (8 canonical paths, 6 conventions)
raw_admissibility.raw_66: ai-native error trailers — sentinel emits reason+fix trailer
raw_admissibility.raw_80: sentinel evolution backward-compat — --legacy-5 flag retains N/5; both honesty_5_5 and honesty_6_6 emitted in both modes
raw_admissibility.raw_71: cross-repo write policy — CANON defense gap is observed, not auto-fixed

axes.decision: Add precondition (f) check_precondition_f(repo_path) surveying 8 canonical paths (top-level SECURITY*, doc/security/*, docs/security/*, design/security/*, design/SECURITY_AUDIT.md, design/hexa_sim/SECURITY_AUDIT.md, state/security_*.json, tool/security_*); PASS if ANY exists.
axes.rationale: Each of the 4 repos expresses defense in a different convention; a single-path check would be biased. Multi-path PASS-any normalizes across tool/state/doc shapes.

changes: Added check_precondition_f bash 3.2 function (8 canonical paths surveyed)
Per-repo loop tracks pass_5 and pass_6 separately; TOTAL_HONESTY_5_5 and TOTAL_HONESTY_6_6 always emitted
Repo health table denominator switches with mode
Aggregate section reports both honesty_5_5 and honesty_6_6
Sentinel extended: honesty_pass=N/R honesty_5_5=X honesty_6_6=Y mode=5|6
Added raw 66 trailer comment (reason+fix) after sentinel

output_per_repo.nexus: 6/6 (a=PASS b=PASS c=PASS d=PASS e=PASS f=PASS) — defense via design/hexa_sim/SECURITY_AUDIT.md + tool/security_scan.hexa
output_per_repo.CANON: 5/6 (a=PASS b=PASS c=PASS d=PASS e=PASS f=FAIL) — no defense surface in any of 8 canonical paths
output_per_repo.anima: 6/6 (a=PASS b=PASS c=PASS d=PASS e=PASS f=PASS) — defense via state/security_roi_audit.json
output_per_repo.hexa-lang: 5/6 (a=PASS b=PASS c=PASS d=FAIL e=PASS f=PASS) — defense via doc/security/os-level-enforcement-limits.md; (d) ceiling holds

axes.sentinel: __ATLAS_CROSS_REPO_DASHBOARD__ repos=4 total_atlas_lines=65450 total_facts=28848 honesty_pass=2/4 honesty_5_5=3 honesty_6_6=2 mode=6
__ATLAS_CROSS_REPO_DASHBOARD__ repos=4 total_atlas_lines=65450 total_facts=28848 honesty_pass=3/4 honesty_5_5=3 honesty_6_6=2 mode=5
axes.verdict: Legacy mode reproduces prior cycle's reading exactly (3/4 honesty_pass). Backward-compat verified.

changes: Repo health table now N/6 with Δ-vs-prior-N/5 column
New §'Precondition (f) Defense Surface Declared' with definition / rationale / per-repo evidence / raw 73 admissibility note / raw 71 caveat
Improvements section marks (f) as done, queues (g) atlas-tracked @F security_* facts as next extension

tier_1.kind: dashboard updated to N/6 with (f) section

tier_1.backward_compat: honesty_pass= field preserved; --legacy-5 reproduces prior reading

fixpoint.ceiling: hexa-lang d=FAIL (OPT-A); CANON f=FAIL (defense surface gap, observed-not-prescribed)
fixpoint.next_action: ω-extension (g) atlas-tracked @F security_* facts (deeper Honesty F-fact ontology integration); CANON defense-surface population is a separate user-owned decision per raw 71.

surprising_finding.observation: hexa-lang gained (f) PASS via doc/security/ which lifts it from 4/5 → 5/6 numerator-wise, but its denominator also grew, so the PARTIAL verdict survives unchanged. Meanwhile CANON — the only repo with FULL atlas SSOT (own atlas/atlas.n6, not symlinked) — is the unique repo that FAILS (f). The 'most atlas-pure' repo is the 'least defense-declared' repo.
surprising_finding.interpretation: Atlas/SSOT discipline and security-posture declaration are orthogonal axes; high score on one does not predict the other. This validates (f) as a genuinely new dimension rather than a proxy for existing preconditions.
